LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ORIENT BANCORPORATION AND BANK OF THE ORIENT'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT THE COMPLAINT COMES WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT OR THAT THE SIGNED ARBITRATION AGREEMENT MEETS THE ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHCARE SERVICES, INC. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83, FACTORS. (DECLARATION OF SHEW-WOO IN SUPPORT OF PETITION, 6-8, EXHS. A-C.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CARRIED HIS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS UNCONSCIONABLE. EVEN ASSUMING THAT THE LIMITATION OF THE SCOPE OF THE MATTERS TO BE ARBITRATED INTRODUCES SOME RELATIVELY MINOR DEGREE OF SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY BY FOCUSING ON CLAIMS MOST LIKELY TO BE RAISED BY AN EMPLOYEE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY-OR AT LEAST THE DEGREE OF PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY NECESSARY TO RENDER THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT UNENFORCEABLE IN LIGHT OF EVIDENCE CONCERNING THE PARTIES' RELATIVE BARGAINING POWER, THE COURSE OF NEGOTIATIONS OVER TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT, AND THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ALERTED TO THE ARBITRATION REQUIREMENT IN THE OFFER LETTER. THE PROCEEDINGS ARE STAYED UNTIL ARBITRATION IS COMPLETED. (CCP 1281.4.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Judicial Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT M. STILLMAN OF THE MCGUINN, HILLSMAN & PALEFSKY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-9292 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DALENE R. BRAMER & ATTY. ANDREW M. SPURCHISE OF THE LITTLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 399-8438 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ORIENT BANCORPORATION AND BANK OF THE ORIENT's Petition To Compel Arbitration IS Granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff does not dispute that the complaint comes within the scope of the Arbitration Agreement or that the signed Arbitration Agreement meets the Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, factors. (Declaration of Shew-Woo In Support of Petition, 6-8, Exhs. A-C.) Plaintiff has not carried his burden of demonstrating the Arbitration Agreement is unconscionable. Even assuming that the limitation of the scope of the matters to be arbitrated introduces some relatively minor degree of substantive unconscionability by focusing on claims most likely to be raised by an employee, Plaintiff has not demonstrated procedural unconscionability-or at least the degree of procedural unconscionability necessary to render the Arbitration Agreement unenforceable in light of evidence concerning the parties' relative bargaining power, the course of negotiations over terms of employment, and the fact that Plaintiff was alerted to the arbitration requirement in the offer letter. The proceedings are stayed until arbitration is completed. (CCP 1281.4.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581 =302/pjb
